ogTitle: Cocamba vs Cochranes Creek: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cocamba and Cochranes Creek.
heading: Cocamba vs Cochranes Creek — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cocamba, Victoria and Cochranes Creek, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cochranes Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Cocamba (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cocamba or Cochranes Creek safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cocamba has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Cochranes Creek scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cocamba scores 57/100 for bushfire risk. Cochranes Creek scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cocamba or Cochranes Creek?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cochranes Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
